_comment: Curated, ordered interpretability reading list (broad scope: XAI + mechanistic interp + ML transparency). 'order' sets the explicit learning sequence. 'provides'/'requires' are [slug, display name] concept edges forming the prerequisite graph; concepts that no paper provides (e.g. neural-networks) are treated as assumed background and do not gate ordering. Prose (summary/intuition/depth) is generated by the local LLM at email time.

papers.title: The Mythos of Model Interpretability
papers.abstract: Surveys what 'interpretability' actually means, untangling distinct goals (trust, causality, transferability, fairness) and properties (transparency vs post-hoc explanation). A conceptual map for the whole field.

papers.title: "Why Should I Trust You?": Explaining the Predictions of Any Classifier (LIME)
papers.abstract: LIME explains any classifier's individual prediction by fitting a simple, interpretable surrogate model locally around the instance using perturbed samples.

papers.title: A Unified Approach to Interpreting Model Predictions (SHAP)
papers.abstract: SHAP unifies feature-attribution methods under Shapley values from cooperative game theory, giving additive attributions with desirable uniqueness guarantees.

papers.title: Deep Inside Convolutional Networks: Visualising Image Classification Models and Saliency Maps
papers.abstract: Introduces gradient-based saliency maps: the gradient of the class score w.r.t. the input pixels highlights which pixels most influence the prediction.

papers.title: SmoothGrad: removing noise by adding noise
papers.abstract: Sharpens noisy gradient saliency maps by averaging gradients over many noisy copies of the input.

papers.title: Axiomatic Attribution for Deep Networks (Integrated Gradients)
papers.abstract: Derives attribution from two axioms (sensitivity, implementation invariance) and integrates gradients along a path from a baseline to the input.

papers.title: Learning Important Features Through Propagating Activation Differences (DeepLIFT)
papers.abstract: Attributes predictions by propagating differences from a reference activation, addressing gradient saturation issues.

papers.title: Grad-CAM: Visual Explanations from Deep Networks via Gradient-based Localization
papers.abstract: Uses gradients flowing into the last conv layer to produce a coarse class-discriminative heatmap localizing the regions a CNN used.

papers.title: Sanity Checks for Saliency Maps
papers.abstract: Shows several popular saliency methods are insensitive to model weights or labels, failing basic sanity checks — a cautionary, must-read on evaluation.

papers.title: Anchors: High-Precision Model-Agnostic Explanations
papers.abstract: Explains predictions with high-precision if-then rules ('anchors') that locally guarantee the prediction holds regardless of other features.

papers.title: Counterfactual Explanations without Opening the Black Box
papers.abstract: Proposes counterfactual explanations: the smallest change to inputs that would flip the decision, supporting actionable recourse and GDPR-style explanation.

papers.title: Understanding Black-box Predictions via Influence Functions
papers.abstract: Uses influence functions from robust statistics to trace a prediction back to the training points most responsible for it.

papers.title: Interpretability Beyond Feature Attribution: Concept Activation Vectors (TCAV)
papers.abstract: TCAV quantifies how much a human-defined concept (e.g. 'stripes') influences a class prediction, using directional derivatives along concept activation vectors.

papers.title: Network Dissection: Quantifying Interpretability of Deep Visual Representations
papers.abstract: Aligns individual hidden units with human-labeled visual concepts to measure how disentangled and interpretable a network's representations are.

papers.title: Attention is not Explanation
papers.abstract: Shows attention weights often do not correlate with feature importance and can be altered without changing predictions — questioning attention as explanation.

papers.title: What Does BERT Look At? An Analysis of BERT's Attention
papers.abstract: Probes BERT's attention heads and finds some specialize in syntactic relations, introducing probing as a lens on what representations encode.

papers.title: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
papers.abstract: The circuits agenda: neural networks can be reverse-engineered into meaningful features and the weighted connections (circuits) that compute them.

papers.title: A Mathematical Framework for Transformer Circuits
papers.abstract: Reverse-engineers small attention-only transformers, introducing the residual-stream view, QK/OV circuits, and induction heads.

papers.title: Toy Models of Superposition
papers.abstract: Explains superposition: networks pack more features than neurons by representing them as nearly-orthogonal directions, motivating sparse decomposition.

papers.title: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning
papers.abstract: Uses sparse autoencoders to decompose model activations into many monosemantic, human-interpretable features, addressing superposition.

papers.title: Locating and Editing Factual Associations in GPT (ROME)
papers.abstract: Uses causal/activation tracing to locate where GPT stores a fact, then edits the responsible weights to change it — bridging interpretability and editing.
